author: observation
summary: Use memory patterns to evolve TEOF’s core guidance.

insight: Treat memory/log + reflections as a training set: mine patterns (stalled plans, guard failures, breakthrough insights) and codify them into guardrails, onboarding briefs, and conductor prompts.
Incoming agents shouldn’t read every raw entry; give them plan-scoped summaries distilled from those patterns so inheritance stays lightweight but grounded in real history.
Keep expanding the core architecture by combining synthesized memory patterns with existing design principles (L0–L6, systemic axes) and real-world coordination lessons.
